a_image.alt: Synthwave style mood board — Retro Subgenre · 1980s

sections.a_description: Neon grids stretching toward a glowing horizon under a purple-pink sky — the unmistakable aesthetic of 1980s retrofuturism brought to the screen. Synthwave design channels the chrome-and-laser visual language of Tron, Blade Runner, and the album art of Kavinsky and The Midnight into vivid digital interfaces dripping with hot pink, electric cyan, and deep violet darkness.

Synthwave aesthetics dominate music streaming sites, retro gaming platforms, and creative portfolio pages that want to radiate nocturnal cool. You'll find it on electronic music producer homepages, vaporwave merchandise stores, indie game launch pages, and nightlife event sites — anywhere that wants to feel like a midnight drive through a neon-lit city that never existed.

Deep violet-black background (#0d0221) with a neon sunset gradient horizon — hot pink (#ff2975) through magenta (#f222ff) to electric cyan (#00fff0). The card root uses a vertical gradient from deep indigo (#0d0221) at top to dark purple (#1a0533) at bottom. A pseudo-element creates the classic synthwave horizon — a horizontal gradient line near the bottom fading from transparent to hot pink to transparent, evoking the perspective grid receding to the horizon. The .inner panel is semi-transparent with a faint neon pink border and subtle glow. Text is primarily light lavender (#e0d0ff) with headings in bright white or hot neon pink. Buttons glow neon on hover with box-shadow halos. Set in Orbitron for headings and eyebrow — a geometric, square-proportioned sans-serif that channels retro-futuristic chrome lettering and sci-fi movie titles. Body text uses Exo 2 — a contemporary geometric sans-serif with a technical, futuristic feel that remains highly readable at small sizes. Eyebrow in Orbitron at weight 700, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, 11px. Heading in Orbitron at weight 900, uppercase, 1px letter-spacing, 32px, with a neon pink text-shadow glow. Body in Exo 2 at weight 400, line-height 1.7, 15px. Buttons in Orbitron, uppercase, weight 700, 1px letter-spacing. Primary button has a hot-pink-to-magenta gradient with cyan glow on hover. Secondary is transparent with neon pink border. Alternatives: Audiowide (wider retro-tech), Rajdhani (geometric Devanagari-influenced), Michroma (ultra-wide sci-fi).
{What this palette represents — the core identity}

The card root uses a vertical gradient from deep indigo (#0d0221) at top to dark purple (#1a0533) at bottom. The .inner panel is semi-transparent with a faint neon pink border and subtle glow. Text is primarily light lavender (#e0d0ff) with headings in bright white or hot neon pink.
